text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech breadth held, energy faded, small caps outperformed.
The tape stayed risk-on with 4 major indices at 0.00% on the snapshot, while QQQ carried the cleanest 20d trend at +7.63% and IWM held +6.20% ytd. Flow still favored cyclicals and higher-beta names: XLE volume ran 1.2x, IWM 1.2x, and SPY stayed light at 0.9x, which reads like selective accumulation, not broad de-risking. 

INDICES — VIX was 16.7, down 0.0pt versus the snapshot. Return rank: QQQ +0.00%, DIA +0.00%, SPY +0.00%, IWM +0.00%; STYLE: large = small by 0.00pp. Biggest cross-asset move was USO +7.66% over 20d, while GLD was -3.75% and UUP was +0.91%; SPY and QQQ were not below their 200-day MAs in the data.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio was 0.9x and QQQ was not flagged in the volume breadth table, so both read as muted versus the average tape. Gaining attention was DIA 1.5x, IWM 1.2x, RSP 1.2x, XLE 1.2x, XLP 1.1x, and XLI 1.1x; being ignored was GLD 0.8x, SLV 0.7x, XLY 0.9x, XLV 0.7x, and XLRE 0.7x. With price flat and volume mixed, this is a low-conviction, selective-risk bid rather than a full accumulation day.

SECTORS — XLK Technology +0.00%, XLI Industrials +0.00%, XLC Communications +0.00%, XLF Financials +0.00%, XLU Utilities +0.00%, XLY Discretionary +0.00%, XLRE Real Estate +0.00%, XLP Staples +0.00%, XLB Materials +0.00%, XLE Energy +0.00%, XLV Healthcare +0.00%. Top 2 industry ETFs were DRAM +5.49% and IBUY +1.07%; bottom 2 were WCLD -1.50% and BOAT -0.74%.

THE 7s — Semi +20.48%, Fab +8.41%, Health +5.02%, Energy +2.14%, Old Staples +2.89%. Bottom 5: Power -4.40%, New Metals -4.77%, Buildout -5.64%, Housing -9.18%, Old Metals -10.45%. Strongest tickers were Semi +20.48% and Fab +8.41%; weakest were Old Metals -10.45% and Housing -9.18%.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026, with hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and the stance split is Hawk 6 / Neutral 5 / Dove 4. USD liquidity stress is 28/100, labeled Normal, with SOFR Dispersion at the 1st percentile as the most-stressed component; ON-RRP was just $3.3B and TED spread was 0.09%.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR was 0.72 and QQQ PCR was 1.18, so QQQ showed more protection demand than SPY. SPY skew was +4.8% and QQQ skew was +9.3%, while VIX at 16.7 sat below its 20d MA of 17.6; MOVE at 79.7 was elevated versus 74.0, keeping bond-vol stress on the board.

CROSS-ASSET — XLE volume at 1.2x versus XLE at 0.00% and USO +7.66% over 20d says energy participation is still hotter than sector price action. GLD -3.75% and UUP +0.91% point to dollar strength pressuring gold.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 64.9/100, or Greed 64.9. Top movers were DRAM +5.49% in Industries, XOM +1.30% in Energy, and EWH +1.53% in Countries; 20d momentum best was Semi +20.48% and worst was Old Metals -10.45%, while sector best was XLK +11.47% and worst was XLB -3.66%. INTU at 6.1x volume in Software was accumulation-sized, while IBM at 3.6x in Cloud was attention without price confirmation.

WATCH — XOM +1.30% needs a look because USO is +7.66% on 20d and energy volume is 1.2x. INTU +0.00% matters because 6.1x volume in Software is a real signal despite flat price. QQQ at 0.00% deserves monitoring because PCR 1.18 and skew +9.3% sit near a positioning stress point.
